hands_on_tasks: Reopen/rebuild/travel ten times with one subscription invariant

nodes.name: UI Model Projection, Events, and MVVM
nodes.summary: Project authoritative gameplay into local display-ready snapshots/deltas while emitting validated intents and bounding pull/Tick work.
nodes.why_it_matters: Direct widget polling and duplicated gameplay truth create stale presentation, authority errors and population-scaled CPU cost.

version_notes.label: MVVM Beta/plugin-dependent
version_notes.details: Architecture works without MVVM; FieldNotify/plugin behaviour requires target verification.

hands_on_tasks: Event-driven HUD surviving possession/travel and server rejection
interview_questions: How should gameplay state reach HUD?

nodes.name: Responsive UI Layout, Localisation, and Accessibility
nodes.summary: Reason from desired size and parent constraints through anchors, DPI, safe zones, culture-aware text and accessible interaction.
nodes.why_it_matters: Fixed English-only Canvas layouts fail across aspect, device, culture, input and user needs.

version_notes.label: UE5/platform-sensitive
version_notes.details: Platform accessibility and device safe regions require target hardware tests.

hands_on_tasks: Responsive pseudo-localised panel across target layouts and devices

nodes.name: UI Hit Testing, Focus, Navigation, and CommonUI
nodes.summary: Coordinate per-user focus, hit testing, navigation, modal restoration and one owner for activatable input configuration.
nodes.why_it_matters: Invisible interceptors and competing input-mode owners create controller soft-locks and local multiplayer failures.

version_notes.label: CommonUI plugin/version-sensitive
version_notes.details: Enhanced Input integration must be pinned to UE5.3–UE5.6 branch.

hands_on_tasks: Modal navigation with removed/disabled focus target and split-screen

nodes.name: Virtualised Lists, Async UI Assets, and World Widgets
nodes.summary: Treat rows as recycled views, guard async results by stable identity/generation and scale world widgets through relevance/redraw policy.
nodes.why_it_matters: Stale rows/icons and unbounded per-actor render targets are common correctness and scaling failures.

version_notes.label: UE5 target
version_notes.details: Exact callbacks/rendering settings require target APIs and workload traces.

hands_on_tasks: 1000-row delayed icon test and 100-nameplate comparison

nodes.name: UI Invalidation, Rendering, and Performance Diagnosis
nodes.summary: Classify model/update/layout/paint/draw/asset/GPU cost and use invalidation, volatility, virtualisation or Retainers only against evidence.
nodes.why_it_matters: Cargo-cult caching moves cost into render targets/latency while the real binding, layout, asset or overdraw bottleneck remains.

version_notes.label: UE5.7 guidance / UE5.6 tooling
version_notes.details: Conceptual optimisation source exceeds target; verify implementation and UI in branch.

hands_on_tasks: Before/after trace of 1000-item screen with one-variable experiments
source_conflicts: UE5.7 optimisation details cannot establish identical target implementation.

nodes.name: Audio Sources, Parameters, and Component Lifetime
nodes.summary: Choose Wave, Cue or MetaSound and one-shot versus owned AudioComponent with explicit loop, attachment, parameter and teardown policy.
nodes.why_it_matters: Restarted loops, auto-destroy misuse and pooled stale Components create duplicate, missing and detached audio.

version_notes.label: UE5/version-sensitive MetaSounds
version_notes.details: Core sources stable; exact MetaSound graph/API and Component flags require target checks.

hands_on_tasks: Wave/Cue/MetaSound comparison with owned loop and failure injection

nodes.name: Audio Spatialisation, Concurrency, and Voice Admission
nodes.summary: Map listener-relative attenuation/focus/occlusion into semantic concurrency, priority, stealing and virtual voice policy.
nodes.why_it_matters: A requested source may be inaudible because of listener gain or admission; uncontrolled emitters overwhelm voices and queries.

version_notes.label: Platform/plugin-sensitive
version_notes.details: Spatialisation plugins, voice budgets and exact resolution options vary.

hands_on_tasks: Force reject/steal/virtualise across multiple owners and listeners

nodes.name: Sound Classes, Mixes, Submix Routing, and DSP
nodes.summary: Separate semantic category control from actual dry/send signal routing and per-source versus shared submix DSP.
nodes.why_it_matters: Undocumented routes cause silent/doubled signals, stuck ducking and per-voice effects that scale badly.

version_notes.label: UE5; plugin effects sensitive
version_notes.details: Submix core is UE5.6 sourced; legacy/modulation/effect fields require target check.

hands_on_tasks: Build category settings, overlapping duck and documented effect/send graph

nodes.name: Audio Timing, Quartz, Streaming, and Memory
nodes.summary: Distinguish frame/buffer/sample timing and asset/chunk/decoded residency while balancing latency, I/O, cache and memory.
nodes.why_it_matters: Loaded assets can miss cold playback, cache churn can underrun and game timers cannot guarantee musical/sample timing.

version_notes.label: Highly version/platform-sensitive
version_notes.details: Maintained Quartz/cache guidance resolves UE5.7; pin target APIs/CVars/codecs.

common_bugs: Game Timer used for beat accuracy
hands_on_tasks: Stress long streams plus impact burst under policy/cache variants
source_conflicts: Maintained later-version defaults cannot establish target behaviour.

nodes.name: Networked Audio Presentation, Debugging, and Profiling
nodes.summary: Replicate semantics rather than Components, deduplicate prediction and diagnose request-to-output while profiling populations, DSP, cache and callback headroom.
nodes.why_it_matters: Multicast-only audio fails late join and duplicates owners; stage-blind tuning misses admission, routing or data failures.

version_notes.label: Project/platform-sensitive
version_notes.details: Console CVars and audio/network transport details require target tests.

hands_on_tasks: Two-client late-join/dedup test and 100-emitter profiling report

nodes.name: Niagara Systems, Emitters, Stack, and Parameters
nodes.summary: Model one effect as ordered System, Emitter and Particle scripts whose typed scoped attributes are consumed by Renderers.
nodes.why_it_matters: Wrong group, namespace or later attribute writer creates plausible-looking but incorrect simulation that is hard to diagnose.

version_notes.label: UE5.6 core
version_notes.details: Stack groups and execution states target-pinned; maintained overview can include later features.

hands_on_tasks: Document and perturb every attribute writer in a multi-Emitter impact

nodes.name: Niagara CPU/GPU Simulation and Gameplay Data Flow
nodes.summary: Choose simulation target from population, supported modules/Data Interfaces, CPU interaction/readback, bounds and full CPU/GPU budget while feeding typed presentation data.
nodes.why_it_matters: GPU simulation does not remove CPU/submission cost, and readback or unsupported data paths create latency and correctness traps.

version_notes.label: UE5.6 feature-sensitive
version_notes.details: Data Channel and DI/module target support require exact branch verification.

hands_on_tasks: Compare CPU/GPU impacts and individual Components versus Data Channel records

nodes.name: Niagara Component Lifetime, Attachment, Pre-cull, and Pooling
nodes.summary: Own one-shot/persistent Components explicitly and treat auto-destroy, pre-cull and pool release as boundaries requiring null/stale-safe reset logic.
nodes.why_it_matters: Retained pooled pointers, stale parameters/attachment and assumptions about completion cause cross-effect corruption and gameplay coupling.

version_notes.label: UE5 target API-sensitive
version_notes.details: Spawn overload and pool methods vary; pre-cull is presentation-only.

hands_on_tasks: Stress 1000 pooled one-shots and attached persistent trail with reset assertions

nodes.name: Niagara Bounds, Effect Types, Significance, and Scalability
nodes.summary: Use correct local/dynamic bounds and semantic Effect Type significance to cull or degrade instances predictably by platform and budget.
nodes.why_it_matters: Small bounds pop, huge bounds defeat culling, and indiscriminate budgets remove critical readable feedback or thrash instances.

version_notes.label: UE5.6 core; controls evolve
version_notes.details: Effect Type API target-pinned; maintained budgeting docs may exceed target.

hands_on_tasks: Inject bounds and budget failures across Combat/Environment/UI Effect Types

nodes.name: Niagara Collision, Renderers, Materials, and Overdraw
nodes.summary: Keep collision cosmetic and select Sprite/Mesh/Ribbon/Light/Component rendering from geometry, submission, lighting, sorting and pixel cost.
nodes.why_it_matters: Particle count hides full-screen translucent overdraw, particle-light multiplication, Component Ticks and delayed/partial collision data.

version_notes.label: Maintained later rendering/collision docs
version_notes.details: GPU ray-tracing collision experimental; exact target renderer features require proof.

hands_on_tasks: Compare renderers/materials/collision while retaining one authoritative gameplay hit

nodes.name: Niagara Networking, Debugging, Profiling, and Lightweight Emitters
nodes.summary: Replicate semantics rather than Components, diagnose stack/state/cull causes and profile instance, simulation, submission, GPU graphics and memory before evaluating UE5.4+ lightweight Emitters.
nodes.why_it_matters: Predicted duplicates and stage-blind optimisation break presentation while later features are often assumed available without target proof.

version_notes.label: UE5.4+ / tool-sensitive
version_notes.details: Lightweight Emitters introduced UE5.4; maintained UE5.7 feature set cannot be assumed for target.

hands_on_tasks: Profile 1/20/100 effects and validate dedup/late join/lightweight candidate
source_conflicts: Maintained lightweight feature set exceeds some UE5.4–UE5.6 branches.

nodes.name: Scripting Runtime Adoption and Native API Boundaries
nodes.summary: Adopt an additional runtime only for a concrete need and expose coarse typed semantic commands/snapshots/events rather than raw engine ownership.
nodes.why_it_matters: A broad chatty API multiplies lifetime, thread, platform, security and maintenance risk across every scripted feature.

version_notes.label: Third-party/plugin-dependent
version_notes.details: No Lua/C# runtime path is assumed as Epic baseline; pin commit/runtime/platform.

common_bugs: Plugin README treated as support guarantee
hands_on_tasks: Design Project 10 semantic façade and adoption decision matrix

nodes.name: Lua Tables, Metatables, Coroutines, Errors, and C API
nodes.summary: Use Lua's dynamic table/closure model and virtual C API stack with explicit protected-call, registry, userdata and coroutine discipline.
nodes.why_it_matters: Hidden metamethod calls, retained closures, unbalanced stacks and illegal yield/error paths corrupt integration correctness.

version_notes.label: Lua-version-sensitive
version_notes.details: Language chapter uses Lua 5.4; embedded plugin may use Lua 5.1/LuaJIT/other.

hands_on_tasks: Protected native binding with stack/error/yield tests

nodes.name: Lua–UObject Lifetime, GC, Reload, and Plugin Ecosystems
nodes.summary: Bridge separate Lua/Unreal collectors through native ownership, wrapper invalidation and deterministic callback/registry teardown while evaluating exact plugin commits.
nodes.why_it_matters: Cross-runtime cycles, stale wrappers and old reload-generation callbacks survive travel and crash native code.

version_notes.label: Plugin/commit-sensitive
version_notes.details: UnLua/sluaunreal UE5 claims require exact UE5.3–UE5.6 prototype and package proof.

hands_on_tasks: Destroy/travel/reload under callbacks/coroutines and prove invalidation

nodes.name: C# Managed Lifetime, Marshalling, Delegates, and Async
nodes.summary: Bridge moving managed GC and native Unreal ownership with explicit ABI, handles, deterministic resource/callback teardown and Game Thread dispatch.
nodes.why_it_matters: Wrong bool/layout, unrooted callbacks, long pinning, unmanaged exceptions and Task continuations cause data corruption or crashes.

version_notes.label: .NET/runtime/plugin-sensitive
version_notes.details: General .NET facts apply; exact host/runtime/API surface must be pinned.

common_bugs: Delegate collected while native retains pointer
hands_on_tasks: Round-trip ABI record and invalidatable callback/Task wrapper

nodes.name: C# Runtime Hosting, UnrealCLR, AOT, and Packaging
nodes.summary: Evaluate a specific .NET host/integration across runtime configuration, API coverage, assembly lifecycle, JIT/AOT, staging, symbols and platform policy.
nodes.why_it_matters: Desktop editor success says little about packaged server, console/mobile, trimming, dynamic loading or long-term servicing.

version_notes.label: Highly plugin/platform-sensitive
version_notes.details: UnrealCLR is one third-party snapshot; .NET AOT support does not imply plugin/console support.

hands_on_tasks: Produce and test Project 10 runtime/platform/deployment matrix

nodes.name: Scripting Reload, Security, Networking, Debugging, and Profiling
nodes.summary: Treat reload as state migration, expose allow-listed authority-safe APIs and correlate mixed stacks/generations while measuring boundary, GC and retention costs.
nodes.why_it_matters: File replacement leaves old closures/assemblies/callbacks, full reflection defeats sandboxing and single-stack profiles hide boundary failures.

version_notes.label: Project/runtime-sensitive
version_notes.details: Reload/sandbox/debug capabilities vary by plugin and shipping platform policy.

hands_on_tasks: Project 10 reload/security/fault matrix and comparative benchmarks

nodes.name: Template Deduction, Forwarding, SFINAE, and Concepts
nodes.summary: Design readable generic APIs using deduction/reference collapsing, one-use perfect forwarding and explicit compile-time requirements.
nodes.why_it_matters: Universal-reference hijacking, double forwarding and opaque substitution failures cause correctness and maintainability problems.

version_notes.label: C++11–20; project-sensitive
version_notes.details: Concepts require C++20; target UE branch/compiler policy must be pinned.

hands_on_tasks: Forwarding factory and SFINAE/concept diagnostic comparison

nodes.name: Lambda Capture Lifetime and Callable Type Erasure
nodes.summary: Select explicit safe captures and choose templates, function pointers, type-erased wrappers or Unreal delegates from lifetime/runtime semantics.
nodes.why_it_matters: Delayed captures dangle or retain worlds, while unnecessary owning erasure adds copies/allocations/indirection and empty-call hazards.

version_notes.label: C++11+ / UE delegate-sensitive
version_notes.details: std::function reference-return rules changed in C++23; target remains earlier.

hands_on_tasks: Delayed capture failure matrix and callable benchmark

nodes.name: C++ Object Layout, Cache Locality, Arenas, and Pools
nodes.summary: Reason from size/alignment/padding and access patterns to AoS/SoA/chunk layouts and lifetime-specific allocator choices.
nodes.why_it_matters: Raw-byte assumptions corrupt schemas while unmeasured layouts/pools trade simple ownership for padding, escapes, reset bugs and retained memory.

version_notes.label: ABI/hardware/toolchain-sensitive
version_notes.details: PMR is C++17; exact ABI/cache constants/Unreal allocator policy require target proof.

hands_on_tasks: Layout/AoS/SoA and arena/pool comparative lab

nodes.name: C++ Data Races, Mutexes, Condition Variables, and Atomics
nodes.summary: Establish happens-before for compound invariants using ownership/mutexes and use atomics/memory order only for a written publication/counter protocol.
nodes.why_it_matters: Data races are UB; volatile, several atomics or notifications without predicate state do not create correct synchronisation.

version_notes.label: C++11+ / platform-sensitive performance
version_notes.details: Language guarantees stable; atomic cost/lock-free status differ by target.

hands_on_tasks: Race/missed-wake/release-acquire/false-sharing lab

nodes.name: Task Graphs, Parallel Partitioning, Cancellation, and Merge
nodes.summary: Express DAG dependencies over immutable/partitioned work, local outputs and deterministic merge without blocking workers or violating Game Thread affinity.
nodes.why_it_matters: Tiny/chatty tasks, shared-output locks, stale captures and wait cycles erase parallel gains or deadlock/crash after travel.

version_notes.label: UE5 version-sensitive
version_notes.details: Maintained Tasks docs include UE5.5 busy-wait change; pin target API/wait behaviour.

hands_on_tasks: Snapshot-partition-local-output-deterministic-merge pipeline

nodes.name: ODR, Linkage, Static Initialisation, and Sanitizers
nodes.summary: Diagnose symbols/translation units/modules from ODR and linkage, control global startup, and use ASan/TSan/UBSan as bounded dynamic evidence.
nodes.why_it_matters: Header definitions, hidden templates/exports and global constructor order create build/startup failures that clean rebuilds do not explain.

version_notes.label: Toolchain/platform-sensitive
version_notes.details: Sanitizer/ABI/export support varies; maintained Epic coding page may exceed target branch.

hands_on_tasks: Two-module symbol/static-init failures and sanitizer root-report lab

nodes.name: UE Specifiers and Metadata by Consuming System
nodes.summary: Classify UCLASS/USTRUCT/UFUNCTION/UPROPERTY specifiers and metadata by the engine system that consumes them.
nodes.why_it_matters: Specifier cargo-culting leaks authority, persistence, Blueprint API and editor behaviour into the wrong layer.

version_notes.label: UE5.3-UE5.6 preferred
version_notes.details: Confirmed from UE5.6 specifier pages where accessible; rare metadata keys require target compile/source check.

hands_on_tasks: Specifier contract table and five intentional UHT/specifier failures
source_conflicts: Maintained pages may be dynamically rendered or later than target branch.

nodes.name: Config, SaveGame, Transient, and Duplication Boundaries
nodes.summary: Separate class defaults, config, runtime, save-game, duplicated and transient cache state.
nodes.why_it_matters: Many packaged, PIE and save/load bugs come from assuming one property flag designs the whole persistence path.

version_notes.label: Stable concept, project-sensitive archives
version_notes.details: Flags are documented for UE5.6; consuming save/config/archive behaviour remains project-specific.

common_bugs: Config field without class config setup
hands_on_tasks: Config/save/transient/duplicate matrix in Project 7A extension

nodes.name: UObject Identity, Object APIs, Outer, Packages, Names, and Flags
nodes.summary: Choose NewObject, SpawnActor, lookup, load and duplicate paths by intent while separating Outer identity from retention.
nodes.why_it_matters: Wrong object API or Outer mental model causes missing GC edges, skipped Actor lifecycle, blocking loads and duplicate graph corruption.

version_notes.label: UE5.3-UE5.6 preferred
version_notes.details: Creation concepts stable; exact flags, duplicate rules and lookup/load overloads require target headers/source.

hands_on_tasks: Create/find/load/duplicate object graph and log identity fields

nodes.name: Reflected Interfaces versus Native Abstract Interfaces
nodes.summary: Use UINTERFACE/IInterface for reflected UObject/Blueprint capabilities and ordinary C++ interfaces for pure native boundaries.
nodes.why_it_matters: Interfaces reduce concrete coupling only when the capability contract, state location and Blueprint/native call path are clear.

version_notes.label: Stable UE4/UE5 concept
version_notes.details: UE5.6 interface docs confirm reflected interface structure; exact helper names should compile in target branch.

common_bugs: Blueprint-only implementer called as native virtual
Interface used where Component is clearer
debugging_tools: native versus Blueprint call path fixture
hands_on_tasks: Native and Blueprint implementer through same interface plus pure native interface comparison

nodes.name: UE Logging and Assertion Diagnostics
nodes.summary: Use log categories/verbosity/structured fields and choose check, verify and ensure by invariant and build behaviour.
nodes.why_it_matters: Good diagnostics preserve correlation evidence; wrong assertions crash recoverable paths or remove required side effects.

version_notes.label: UE5.2+ for UE_LOGFMT
version_notes.details: UE5.6 logging/assert docs used; build configuration and reporting differ by target.

hands_on_tasks: Inventory move diagnostics with log, ensure and check rationale

nodes.name: UE Core Vocabulary Types and Non-Owning Lifetimes
nodes.summary: Use TOptional, TVariant, TFunctionRef and view-like APIs to express absence, closed alternatives, synchronous callbacks and contiguous borrowed storage.
nodes.why_it_matters: Vocabulary types make contracts explicit but can create dangling views/callables when stored or used asynchronously.

version_notes.label: UE API/version-sensitive
version_notes.details: Concepts stable; exact UE Core APIs and reflection support require UE5.3-UE5.6 header checks.

hands_on_tasks: Optional/variant/callable/view utility with deliberate escape failure
source_conflicts: API pages may not pin exact target branch in maintained docs.

nodes.name: Async Blueprint Proxy Lifetime and Cancellation
nodes.summary: Design async Blueprint nodes around proxy retention, activation, cancellation, stale-generation rejection, Game Thread completion and cleanup.
nodes.why_it_matters: Async teaching examples often compile while leaking proxies, collecting them early or broadcasting after travel/destruction.

version_notes.label: UE5.3-UE5.6 target, API-sensitive
version_notes.details: Proxy API and Blueprint integration details require target branch compile tests.

hands_on_tasks: Timer/async-load proxy tested against destroy/travel/cancel/race cases

nodes.name: Native GC Bridges and UObject Thread Boundaries
nodes.summary: Use FGCObject or other explicit retention only for native owners that cannot be reflected, and keep off-thread work on plain snapshots with Game Thread revalidation.
nodes.why_it_matters: Native owners and tasks are common places for hidden UObject retention, early collection, stale captures and wrong-thread crashes.

version_notes.label: UE5.3-UE5.6 target, thread/API-sensitive
version_notes.details: FGCObject signatures and task APIs should be checked against target headers.

common_bugs: Raw this captured in deferred lambda
hands_on_tasks: FGCObject retained/released proof plus snapshot-task apply workflow

nodes.name: Replicated Components and UObject Subobjects
nodes.summary: Replicate nested state through owning Actors or Actor Components using explicit component replication, subobject networking support, registration and teardown.
nodes.why_it_matters: Modular gameplay systems often need nested replicated state without full Actor identity, and mistakes cause null references, stale raw pointers, missing updates or crashes after GC.

version_notes.label: UE5.3-UE5.6 target, branch-sensitive
version_notes.details: Actor Component replication is UE5.6-sourced; Object Replication page is maintained beyond target and exact APIs/CVars require branch checks.

common_bugs: Registered subobject not removed before GC
hands_on_tasks: Project 3 Specialist Replication Extension components and dynamic UObject subobject
source_conflicts: SRC-NET-012 maintained page can exceed target docs.

nodes.name: Fast Array and Push-Model Replication
nodes.summary: Use item-level delta replication and dirty reporting only when stable identity, centralised authoritative mutation and measured benefit justify the complexity.
nodes.why_it_matters: High-churn replicated collections are common interview systems, but missing dirty marks or unstable item IDs create silent desynchronisation.

version_notes.label: API/header-sensitive
version_notes.details: FFastArraySerializer docs are an API anchor; exact callbacks and dirty-marking helpers must be checked in target source.

common_bugs: Fast Array used for tiny static array
hands_on_tasks: Project 3 Fast Array inventory/status comparison
source_conflicts: CVar/API names are branch-sensitive.

nodes.name: Replication Graph Scaling and Relevance Lists
nodes.summary: Use persistent graph nodes and shared relevance data to reduce per-Actor/per-connection replication list construction when scale justifies it.
nodes.why_it_matters: Large multiplayer worlds fail from server replication CPU and bandwidth policy before individual gameplay code looks expensive.

version_notes.label: Plugin/project-sensitive
version_notes.details: Replication Graph docs are UE5.6; plugin/platform caveats and exact node APIs require target checks.

hands_on_tasks: Project 3 Replication Graph adoption/rejection memo
interview_questions: What problem does Replication Graph solve?

nodes.name: Iris Replication Awareness
nodes.summary: Recognise Iris as an opt-in experimental replication system and verify project configuration, registered subobjects and fragment requirements before implementation claims.
nodes.why_it_matters: UE5 interviews may mention Iris, but claiming it is automatic or baseline is a version/project error.

version_notes.label: Experimental/opt-in
version_notes.details: Epic Iris docs label the feature experimental/opt-in; exact UE5.3-UE5.6 status and APIs require source/config proof.

common_bugs: Copying Iris-only subobject requirements into Generic project
hands_on_tasks: Project 3 Iris enabled/not-enabled evidence note
interview_questions: How talk about Iris in UE5 interview?
source_conflicts: Maintained Iris pages can resolve beyond target version.

nodes.name: Network Debug CVars and Evidence Workflow
nodes.summary: Use targeted network debug commands/CVars to validate hypotheses about dormancy, NetGUIDs, subobjects, push model, Fast Arrays and packet behaviour.
nodes.why_it_matters: Network bugs are timing-sensitive; broad logging or folklore fixes hide the true authority, mapping or relevancy problem.

version_notes.label: Maintained CVar list, target-sensitive
version_notes.details: Console command availability/names/costs differ by branch/build; query target runtime before use.

common_bugs: Turning on many CVars without hypothesis
hands_on_tasks: Project 3 one-CVar diagnosis note
interview_questions: How do network debug CVars fit diagnosis?
source_conflicts: Maintained CVar docs can exceed UE5.3-UE5.6.

nodes.name: Device Lab Matrix and Run Manifests
nodes.summary: Define a small target-device/scenario matrix and bind every run to build, device, scenario, command line, install mode and output artifacts.
nodes.why_it_matters: Device-only failures are hard to reproduce unless the evidence packet preserves exact identity and environment.

version_notes.label: Platform/build-farm-sensitive
version_notes.details: Device IDs, install modes, logs and artifact paths vary by platform and lab infrastructure.

common_bugs: CI job URL treated as artifact identity
Scenario has no warm-up or exit marker
hands_on_tasks: Project 4/6 Device Lab Automation Extension run manifest
interview_questions: What belongs in a device-lab run manifest?

nodes.name: Gauntlet / RunUnreal Target Automation
nodes.summary: Use UE-aware RunUnreal/Gauntlet workflows where appropriate to launch packaged/editor targets, run automation tests and monitor target output.
nodes.why_it_matters: A device lab needs repeatable target orchestration rather than manual launches, but project scenarios and platform adapters remain explicit work.

version_notes.label: Branch/platform-sensitive
version_notes.details: Gauntlet C# APIs, command classes and deployment support must be verified in the target branch and platform.

common_bugs: Assuming Gauntlet tests the whole game automatically
Copying maintained-doc command without target check
hands_on_tasks: Create a minimal RunUnreal boot or target-automation command note
interview_questions: Where does Gauntlet fit in Unreal automation?
source_conflicts: Public command examples require target environment verification.

nodes.name: Device Lab Telemetry and Artifact Harvesting
nodes.summary: Collect logs, CSV, profile proof, screenshots, selected traces, crash reports and manifests with the correct run identity.
nodes.why_it_matters: Frequent lightweight telemetry detects regressions while selective traces provide causal evidence without overwhelming the lab.

version_notes.label: Trace/command/platform-sensitive
version_notes.details: CSV categories, trace channels, artifact pull locations and profiler support vary by build and platform.

common_bugs: CSV missing because app crashed before flush
Artifact pull failure treated as gameplay fail

nodes.name: Flaky Device Triage and Gate Promotion
nodes.summary: Classify failures as product, infrastructure, flaky device or reporting-only noise, and promote metrics to blocking gates only after variance and ownership are understood.
nodes.why_it_matters: Unclassified red device runs either block real releases with noise or hide deterministic product failures behind reruns.

version_notes.label: Studio-policy-sensitive
version_notes.details: Rerun, quarantine and blocking-threshold policies depend on lab infrastructure, product risk and release phase.

hands_on_tasks: Classify synthetic product, lab, flaky and noisy metric failures
interview_questions: When should an automated metric become release-blocking?
